RequestTemplate.title: RequestTemplate
RequestTemplate.description: Defines the structure and fields for a request workflow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Request Template.

description.description: A more detailed explanation of what the template is used for.

fields.description: Defines the data input fields for the request template.

steps.description: Defines the workflow steps for the request template.

rules.description: Defines the business logic rules for the workflow.

Request.title: Request
Request.description: Represents an instance of a request based on a template.

submissionDate.description: Date and time the request was submitted.

status.description: Current status of the request (e.g., 'Pending', 'In Progress', 'Completed').

data.description: JSON object containing the data entered for the request fields.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a single actionable task assigned to a user, derived from a workflow step.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task document.

requestTitle.description: Denormalized title of the parent request for display purposes.

requestOwnerId.description: The user ID of the person who created the request.

stepId.description: Identifier of the step from the original template.

name.description: Name of the task, inherited from the step definition.

assigneeId.description: The user ID of the person this task is assigned to. Can be null.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the task was completed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the task was created.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the FlowMaster system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User (matches Firebase Auth UID).

skills.description: A list of skills the user possesses.

currentWorkload.description: A numeric value representing the user's current workload.

role.description: The role assigned to the user, controlling their permissions.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a document attached to a request.

filename.description: The name of the document file.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the document (e.g., 'application/pdf').

size.description: The size of the document in bytes.

uploadDate.description: The date and time the document was uploaded.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a comment made on a request.

requestId.description: Reference to the Request this comment belongs to.

authorId.description: The ID of the user who wrote the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was posted.

AuditLog.title: Audit Log
AuditLog.description: Represents a single, immutable audit trail event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the log entry.

requestId.description: The ID of the request this log belongs to.

userId.description: The ID of the user who performed the action.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the event occurred.

details.description: A map of additional data relevant to the action.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification for a user.

definition.description: Stores request templates. Includes template 'id' as the document ID.

params.name: requestTemplateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the request template.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. The 'userId' is the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user (Firebase Auth UID).

definition.description: Stores requests submitted by users. 'submittedBy' field is denormalized and must match the 'userId' path parameter. This achieves authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who submitted the request.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the request.

definition.description: A centralized collection of all actionable tasks. This structure allows for efficient querying of tasks assigned to a specific user across all requests.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier for the task.

definition.description: Stores documents associated with a request. Inherits ownership from the parent request document. The 'requestId' field must match the parent request.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns the request.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the request.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document.

definition.description: Stores comments for a specific request. Access is inherited from the parent request.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The ID of the request being commented on.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique ID of the comment.

definition.description: Stores immutable audit trail records for a specific request. Access is inherited from the parent request.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The ID of the request being audited.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: The unique ID for the audit log entry.

definition.description: Stores notifications for a specific user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The user to whom the notification belongs.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique ID for the notification.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to manage request workflows, templates, requests, steps, users, and documents, focusing on security, scalability, and debuggability. A key change is the introduction of a top-level `/tasks` collection. This denormalizes workflow steps into a centralized, flat list of actionable tasks, which is critical for efficient querying. For example, fetching all tasks assigned to a specific user (`where('assigneeId', '==', userId)`) is now a simple, scalable query on a single collection. This avoids costly and complex collection group queries. Each task document contains denormalized data from its parent request (like `requestTitle` and `requestOwnerId`) to provide sufficient context for display in a user's task list without requiring additional lookups. The original request documents under `/users/{userId}/requests/{requestId}` still maintain a record of their steps, but now include a `taskId` to link back to the primary task document in the `/tasks` collection. This hybrid approach provides both a query-efficient task management system and a clear, hierarchical record of the entire request process.
